data.title: Key deals this week: Savills, Cintas, Aureus Greenway, Papa John's, and more
data.description:
Here's a list of key deals reported across sectors this week:

  • Urgent.ly, Inc (ULY) agreed to be acquired by Agero for $5.50 per share in cash. The transaction will proceed via a tender offer followed by a merger, converting remaining shares into the same $5.50 cash consideration.
  • UK's Savills (SVLPF) to acquire Eastdil Secured Holdings, a real estate investment bank, for an enterprise value of $1,112.5 million (£827 million). Savills shares are down 3.7% in early London trading.
  • Papa John's (PZZA) jumped 19% after a report that Qatari-backed investment fund Irth Capital Management submitted a takeover offer. Irth, with backing from Brookfield Asset Management (BAM), offered to pay $47 a share for the pizza chain.
  • Ondas (ONDS) has acquired airborne defense technologies provider BIRD Aerosystems. Founded in Israel in 2001, BIRD Aerosystems is a developer and provider of airborne missile protection systems and airborne intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) solutions for military, government, and homeland security customers worldwide.
  • Uniform retail firm Cintas (CTAS) to acquire smaller rival UniFirst (UNF) for $310 per share in cash and stock, representing an enterprise value of about $5.5B. UniFirst shares advanced as much as 13% in early trading to $291.87 on Wednesday.
  • La Rosa (LRHC) stock was surging after the real estate and PropTech enterprise announced a non-binding letter of intent to acquire Consensus Core Technologies in an all-stock deal.
  • Aureus Greenway Holdings (AGH) has signed a definitive merger agreement with Powerus, a U.S. Army Special Operations veteran-founded firm developing autonomous drone systems for high-risk military and commercial environments.
  • Universal Health Services (UHS) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Talkspace (TALK) for $5.25 per share. The enterprise value for the transaction is around $835M, which UHS intends to finance with borrowings pursuant to its existing revolving credit facility.

data.title: Japan, South Korea vow to fight forex volatility
data.description:
The finance ministers of Japan and South Korea on Saturday said that they are willing to take appropriate steps to defend their respective currencies, which have sharply depreciated against the U.S. dollar amid ongoing tensions in the Middle East.

Following a meeting in Tokyo, Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama and her South Korean counterpart Koo highlighted the importance of being “vigilant” and “agile” when implementing policy decisions to support economic growth and maintain financial stability.

The ministers of the two Asian neighbors met for the 10th round of the Japan–Korea Finance Ministerial Dialogue on Saturday at a time when the Korean won (USD:KRW) and the Japanese yen (USD:JPY) have reached record lows this month.

With the ongoing U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran weighing on the currencies of countries heavily dependent on imported oil, the ministers raised serious concerns over the rapid decline of their currency values.

"Furthermore, they reaffirmed that they will closely monitor ​foreign exchange markets and continue to take appropriate actions against excessive volatility and disorderly movements in exchange rates," a joint statement from the ministers read.

data.title: Top and bottom quant-rated materials stocks over $10B: Coeur Mining leads 'Strong Buys'
data.description:
As earnings season winds down, investors are shifting focus to updated quant ratings following the latest wave of corporate results. The scores offer a snapshot of how companies rank across key factors such as valuation, growth, profitability, momentum, and estimate revisions after reporting quarterly performance.

Of note, the State Street Global Advisors Materials Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLB) climbed about 10% year to date, leading sector gains even as broader markets remain volatile.

All top-ranked materials stocks have a Strong Buy rating.

Top-quant rated stocks:

  • Coeur Mining, Inc. (CDE), Quant Rating: 4.97, Strong Buy.
  • Glencore plc (GLNCY), Quant Rating: 4.95, Strong Buy.
  • IAMGOLD Corporation (IAG), Quant Rating: 4.94, Strong Buy.
  • Impala Platinum Holdings Limited (IMPUY), Quant Rating: 4.94, Strong Buy.
  • Rio Tinto Group (RIO), Quant Rating: 4.93, Strong Buy.

Lowest quant-rated stocks:

  • Givaudan SA (GVDNY), Quant Rating: 2.07, Sell.
  • RPM International Inc. (RPM), Quant Rating: 1.96, Sell.
  • Symrise AG (SYIEY), Quant Rating: 1.78, Sell.
  • Novozymes A/S (NVZMY), Quant Rating: 1.73, Sell.
  • DSM-Firmenich AG (DSFIY), Quant Rating: 1.43, Strong Sell.

Materials ETFs: (XLB), (VAW), (IYM), (FXZ), (MXI), (RSPM)

data.title: SA Asks: What's the most attractive gold stock right now?
data.description:
What's the most attractive gold stock right now for investors?

Seeking Alpha analysts Andrew Hecht and Agar Capital gave us their picks.

Andrew Hecht: As gold and silver prices increased in late January, I sold all risk positions in gold and silver mining stocks in The Hecht Commodity Report, resulting in significant profits since the long positions were at much lower levels. The sector has not yet reached the late January highs but has consolidated and shows signs that another upward move could be imminent. 

I recommend a diversified approach to gold-mining stocks, using the VanEck Gold Miners (GDX) and VanEck Junior Gold Miners (GDXJ) ETFs, which track a mix of senior and junior gold-mining stocks. For silver, the Global X Silver Miners (SIL) and Amplify Junior Silver Miners (SILJ) ETFs track a selection of senior and junior silver-mining stocks. 

A word of caution: prices are still at historically high levels, which raises the risk of downward corrections.

Agar Capital: At present, Newmont (NEM) would be my first choice for investment. I am highly confident that NEM will exceed its peers because of its favorable valuation relative to the sector and an attractive return on equity of over 20%. I also find their margin-based approach to be extremely attractive since they are generating substantial amounts of free cash flow and utilizing substantially all of it to repurchase shares. 

If I had to select only one stock from the list of gold mining stocks available for investment at present, I would definitely select Newmont. I want to add and emphasize that Newmont is currently the largest gold miner in the world and the only large gold miner included in the S&P 500 (SP500), therefore guaranteeing substantial structural liquidity associated with passive institutional flows, including ETFs.

  • Top Gold Stocks

data.title: Hims & Hers posts best weekly gain after Novo deal
data.description:
Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) rose ~57% this week, marking its best-ever weekly advance after the telehealth firm ended a months-long dispute with Novo Nordisk (NVO) and agreed to sell the pharma giant’s brand-name weight-loss drugs on its platform.

The previous best weekly gain for Hims & Hers (HIMS) was in early May 2025, just after the company partnered with Novo (NVO) to allow its members to access the Danish drugmaker’s weight loss therapy, Wegovy.

That partnership fell apart a month later as Hims & Hers (HIMS) continued to sell low-cost compounded versions of Novo’s (NVO) GLP-1 drugs, Ozempic and Wegovy, on its platform, leading to a bitter feud between the two companies.

The dispute came to a head in February after HIMS’s plans to sell copies of Novo’s newly launched oral obesity therapy, the Wegovy pill, prompted Novo (NVO) to sue the company on allegations of patent infringement.

The latest deal ends the lawsuit and allows the company to sell copies of Ozempic and Wegovy, as well as the Wegovy pill, only for those with a medical necessity as recommended by a doctor.

However, HIMS will no longer advertise compounded versions of those drugs and will start to sell Novo's (NVO) brand-name GLP-1s as early as this month.

Multiple analysts upgraded Hims & Hers (HIMS) in reaction, including Citi, which lifted its rating to Neutral from Sell, noting that the agreement “significantly derisks the story, and as such, we turn more constructive."

However, HIMS remains a Hold-rated stock on Wall Street with only four Buy ratings, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant System, which has a proven track record in outperforming the market, rates HIMS as a Strong Sell.

Notably, SA analysts have largely remained bullish on HIMS, with 11 Buy ratings, three Hold ratings, and only one Sell rating.
